---
title: "Bastian Schulz"
description: "Associate Professor of Economics at Aarhus University. Research on macroeconomics, labor and family economics, search and matching, and inequality."
pagetitle: "Bastian Schulz — Economist, Aarhus University"
image: profile.jpg
image-alt: "Portrait of Bastian Schulz"
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:bastian.schulz@econ.au.dk
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: https://github.com/bsecon/CV/raw/master/CV_Schulz.pdf
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR_ID
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/YOUR_ID
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/bsecon
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

I am an Associate Professor of Economics at the
[Department of Economics and Business Economics, Aarhus University](http://econ.au.dk),
and the [ECONAU](https://econ.au.dk/econau) data manager.
I serve as an Associate Editor at
[Empirical Economics](https://link.springer.com/journal/181).
I hold a PhD in Economics from [LMU Munich](https://www.econ.lmu.de/en).

**Research interests:** macroeconomics, labor and family economics, search
and matching, inequality.

**Affiliations:** [IZA](http://iza.org) ·
[CESifo](http://www.cesifo-group.de/ifoHome/research/Network.html) ·
[RFBerlin](https://www.rfberlin.com) ·
[GLO](https://glabor.org) ·
[PIREAU](https://pireau.au.dk/) ·
[CEFAU](https://econ.au.dk/cefau).

---

## Selected publications

**[Educational Ambition, Marital Sorting, and Inequality](https://github.com/bsecon/Papers/raw/master/AFRSV_September_2025.pdf)**
with Frederik Almar, Benjamin Friedrich, Ana Reynoso, and Rune Vejlin.
*Journal of Labor Economics*, accepted 2025.

**[Firm Productivity, Wages, and Sorting](https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/10.1086/722564)**
with Ben Lochner.
*Journal of Labor Economics*, 42(1), 2024.

**[Labor Market Dynamics with Sorting](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jedc.2023.104776)**
*Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control*, 158, 2024.

[See all publications →](publications.qmd)

---

## Upcoming presentations

- Norwegian School of Economics (NHH), Bergen — November 2026
- BSE Summer Forum, *The Macroeconomics of Labor Markets*, Barcelona — June 2026 *(by coauthor)*
- New Developments in the Macroeconomics of Labor Markets, Nuremberg — June 2026
- Nordic Data Meeting, Uppsala — April 2026
- Rockwool Foundation, Copenhagen — March 2026

---

## News

**January 2026** — [Job Displacement, Remarriage, and Marital Sorting](https://github.com/bsecon/Papers/raw/master/FOS_Jobloss_Marriage.pdf) is under revision for *American Economic Journal: Macroeconomics*.

**November 2025** — New grant from the [Danish e-infrastructure Consortium](https://www.deic.dk/en/front) (DeiC), including computation time at the European supercomputer [LUMI](https://www.lumi-supercomputer.eu/).

**October 2025** — New grant from the [Independent Research Fund Denmark](https://dff.dk/en/about-the-fund/news-and-press/all-news/news-uk/2025/oktober/182-new-ideas-and-original-research-projects-take-flight/) for the project *Disentangling the Interplay of Marriage, Divorce, Fertility, and Work* (joint with Frederik Almar, Edoardo Ciscato, Hanno Foerster, Andrew Shephard, and Guanyi Wang).

**October 2025** — [Educational Ambition, Marital Sorting, and Inequality](https://github.com/bsecon/Papers/raw/master/AFRSV_September_2025.pdf) accepted at *Journal of Labor Economics*.

**April 2025** — Media coverage in [Weekendavisen: *Golddiggermyten*](https://www.weekendavisen.dk/2025-15/ideer/golddiggermyten).

**January 2025** — New working paper: [Families' Career Investments and Firms' Promotion Decisions](https://github.com/bsecon/Papers/raw/master/AFRSV_families_and_firms.pdf) — also out as [NBER WP 33438](https://www.nber.org/papers/w33438), [IZA DP 17367](https://www.iza.org/publications/dp/17653), and [CESifo WP 11659](https://www.cesifo.org/en/publications/2025/working-paper/families-career-investments-and-firms-promotion-decisions).
